meta.title: Skill Graph Arch Brief — Unified Entity Graph
meta.description: Architecture interview for mixing skill graph + content graph + beads + assets as one file-based substrate. Per-lane bucketing with framework attribution.

props.title: One Graph To Rule Them All
props.subtitle: Skill graph + content graph + beads + assets — file-based substrate with framework attribution per node

props.content: The skill graph is proven. Now mix it with every other entity Lev tracks — content, assets, beads, flows, specs, decisions. All file-based. All framework-attributed. All queryable via skill://, content://, bead://, asset://. This brief shows the current bucketing, framework overlaps, and the path to one unified substrate.

**Driver**

The 4-lane operator model is empirically load-bearing. χ=4 (TopoNetX), 95.10% Louvain agreement, hand-authored taxonomy — three independent validations. Owner stack locked (Pydantic→JSON→NetworkX→GraphML). But skill graph is only ONE of many entity types Lev tracks: content (articles, /now pages), assets (images, videos, audio), beads (tasks), flows (flowmind YAMLs), specs (dna/core), decisions (ADRs), handoffs (.lev/pm/handoffs). They all live in the filesystem. They all have frontmatter. They all reference each other via wiki-links. **They are the same graph**.

**Quality Attributes**

- **Correctness**: 7/7 gates pass, χ=4 invariant, Pydantic round-trip stable
- **Scale**: 155 today, 10k after Phase 1 ingest, 100k when beads + assets + content land
- **Performance**: <100ms query at 1k, sharded beyond 10k
- **Cross-kind traversal**: `nx.ancestors(skill://work)` must reach `content://constraint-manifesto` via IMPLEMENTS + REFERENCES edges
- **Framework attribution**: Every skill/feature tagged with source project (OMX, OOO, Lev, Hermes, OpenClaw)
- **Overlap detection**: When 5 frameworks all implement 'doctor/status', the graph shows that convergence

**ADRs (locked + proposed)**

- ADR-001: Owner = Pydantic + JSON + NetworkX + GraphML ✅
- ADR-002: Sidecars may not write to owner ✅
- ADR-003: Promotion = 5 gates + human audit ✅
- ADR-004: `skill://` is canonical runtime URI ✅
- ADR-005: FalkorDB deferred to clawbuddy marketplace ✅
- ADR-006: Cytoscape.js is graph explorer UI library ✅
- ADR-007: Fast-follow ingest = filesystem as graph substrate ✅
- ADR-008: AgentPing gen-ui hosts explorer, exported as lev-now asset ✅
- **ADR-009 (new)**: Single NodeKind enum covers ALL entity types (SKILL, FEATURE, ROLE, LANE, FILE, CONTENT, ASSET, BEAD, FLOW, SPEC, DECISION, HANDOFF)
- **ADR-010 (new)**: `source_project` field is canonical for framework attribution — OMX/OOO/OMC/OMO/Hermes/OpenClaw/Lev
- **ADR-011 (new)**: Cross-kind edges use same EdgeKind enum — REFERENCES, IMPLEMENTS, SUPERSEDES work across SKILL→CONTENT, BEAD→FLOW, etc.

props.label: UNIFIED VISION
props.title: Six Graphs → One Substrate
props.subtitle: Each entity type is a NodeKind; all share the owner stack

props.title: Skill Graph
props.content: **155 nodes today.** 46 skills + 81 features + 24 roles + 4 lanes. Tracks capabilities and their framework origins.

props.title: Content Graph
props.content: **~200 /now pages + articles.** Constraint manifesto, lifecycle venn, 3-part series. Published to here.now. Each a CONTENT node.

props.title: Asset Graph
props.content: **~500 images + videos + diagrams.** `~/.agents/diagrams/`, `levnow/` media, /now renders. Each an ASSET node.

props.title: Beads Graph
props.content: **BD issues + dependencies.** Cross-reference with skills (this bead uses `skill://work`). Each a BEAD node.

props.title: Flow Graph
props.content: **~150 .flow.yaml files.** FlowMind topology already graph-native. Each flow becomes a FLOW node with COMPILES_TO edges.

props.title: Decision Graph
props.content: **ADRs, specs, handoffs.** `.lev/pm/decisions/`, `docs/specs/`, `.lev/pm/handoffs/`. Each a DECISION or HANDOFF node.

props.label: LANE · SHAPE (alignment)
props.title: Shape — Understanding the Problem
props.subtitle: 11 skills · 6 external features · 7 roles · 25 nodes total

props.content:
**Topic**: Requirements discovery, architectural interviewing, decomposing uncertainty.

**Human says**: "Talk it through" / "Help me shape this"

**Runtime surfaces**: arch-interview, work-deepen, deep-interview

**Lev Skills in Shape (11):**

- **`interview`** — Wizard-mode questioning, framework library (SCAMPER, First Principles). **Overlaps:** omx-02 (deep-interview), ooo-01 (Socratic ambiguity). **Origin:** Lev native, influenced by OMX + Ouroboros patterns.
- **`cdo`** — Adaptive multi-agent deliberation (think/debug/parliament). Fractal context loop. **Overlaps:** omx-05 (RALPLAN as consensus step). **Origin:** Lev native, extends tribunal.
- **`prior-art`** — Evidence-first provenance from cass, repo docs, .lev, diagrams. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`cass`** — Wrapper for cass CLI (coding agent session search). **Origin:** Lev integration.
- **`cass-memory`** — Procedural memory, three-layer cognitive. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-research`** — General research router. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`sherlock`** — Deep research intelligence system. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`codebase-archaeology`** — Explore unfamiliar codebases. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`skill-discovery`** — Local skills runtime lookup. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`search`** — Unified search router. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`brainstorming`** — ⚠️ SUPERSEDED by `interview`. **Origin:** Lev (deprecated).

**External Features Implementing Shape (6):**

- **omx-01** `[OMX]` — 4-lane operator model (interview entry) → implemented by `interview`
- **omx-02** `[OMX]` — Deep-interview with mathematical ambiguity scoring → implemented by `interview`
- **ooo-01** `[Ouroboros]` — Socratic interview with ambiguity scoring (threshold 0.2) → implemented by `interview`
- **ooo-03** `[Ouroboros]` — Double Diamond decomposition → partial
- **ooo-11** `[Ouroboros]` — Brownfield auto-detection → partial
- **omo-01** `[OMO]` — Category routing (task classification → mode selection) → partial
**Agent Roles in Shape (7):** explore, analyst, architect, researcher, product-manager, ux-researcher, information-architect

props.label: LANE · PLAN (crystallization)
props.title: Plan — Execution-Ready Commitment
props.subtitle: 11 skills · 3 external features · 4 roles · 18 nodes total

props.content:
**Topic**: Execution-ready plans, canon alignment, gate resolution, graph proposals.

**Human says**: "Turn this into a plan"

**Runtime surfaces**: sdlc-deepen-plan, plan-align-gates-proposal, RALPLAN

**Lev Skills in Plan (11):**

- **`work`** — THE lifecycle skill. Tracked work session (alignment → crystallization → execution → validation). **Overlaps:** omx-04 + ooo-08 (persistent ralph loop). **Origin:** Lev native — the core orchestrator.
- **`lev-plan`** — Plan entity lifecycle in `.lev/pm/plans/`. Required frontmatter, state transitions. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`arch`** — Architecture thinking. ATAM, C4, ADR, quality attributes. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-cdo`** — Lev-specific CDO with architecture context injection. **Overlaps:** omx-05 (RALPLAN consensus). **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`cdo-team`** — Team-driven CDO deliberation (persistent role streams + ephemeral subagents). **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-align`** — `.lev/validation-gates.yaml` loader + gate checker. **Overlaps:** ooo-10 (drift measurement). **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`review-queue`** — Batch entity review dashboard. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-roadmap`** — Operational roadmap refresh with done/not-done triage. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-sdlc`** — SDLC command surface (`lev sdlc *`). **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`planning-workflow`** — Markdown planning methodology. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`writing-plans`** — Spec → plan converter. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`validate`** — Anti-hallucination validation framework. **Origin:** Lev native.

**External Features Implementing Plan (3):**

- **omx-05** `[OMX]` — **RALPLAN consensus planning** → implemented partially by `cdo` + `lev-cdo` + `cdo-team`. The user's favorite. Merges RALPLAN's clean operator UX with Lev's more powerful CDO.
- **ooo-05** `[Ouroboros]` — Evolutionary loop with ontology convergence (0.95 threshold) → partial. eval-harness has produce/judge/compare but no ontology similarity metric yet.
- **ooo-10** `[Ouroboros]` — Drift measurement (50% goal + 30% constraint + 20% ontology) → implemented by `lev-align`.
**Agent Roles in Plan (4):** planner, critic, verifier, product-analyst

props.label: LANE · DELIVER (execution)
props.title: Deliver — Get It Built
props.subtitle: 13 skills · 11 external features · 7 roles · 31 nodes total (biggest lane)

props.content:
**Topic**: Implementation, bugfixing, multi-agent execution, iteration.

**Human says**: "Go build it" / "Fix it"

**Runtime surfaces**: sdlc-exec-validate, ralph, lev-ralph, team, swarm

**Lev Skills in Deliver (13):**

- **`agent-browser`** — Browser automation for web testing, form filling, screenshots, extraction. **Origin:** Lev native (integrated from vercel-labs).
- **`ntm`** — Named Tmux Manager for orchestrating Claude/Codex/Gemini in tmux sessions. **Overlaps:** omx-03 (tmux team lifecycle). **Origin:** Lev integration.
- **`vibing-with-ntm`** — NTM swarm coordination with Agent Mail + Beads + BV. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-workshop`** — Plugin lifecycle (intake → analysis → POC → poly integration). **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`subagent-driven-development`** — Parallel task execution across subagents. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`dispatching-parallel-agents`** — Independent parallel tasks. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`multi-agent-swarm-workflow`** — NTM swarm coordination. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`test-driven-development`** — TDD workflow (red-green-refactor). **Origin:** Lev (imported Martin doctrine).
- **`systematic-debugging`** — Bug investigation discipline. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`multi-pass-bug-hunting`** — Audit-fix-rescan cycle. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`autodev-loop`** — Autonomous dev loop. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`executing-plans`** — Plan execution with review checkpoints. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`simplify`** — Code quality review. **Origin:** Lev native.

**External Features Implementing Deliver (11):**

- **omx-03** `[OMX]` — **Tmux team lifecycle** (N workers, shared task list, commit ledger) → `ntm`. P0 gap: OMX adds team state management, worker health, auto-nudge.
- **omx-04** `[OMX]` — **Ralph persistent loop** with architect verification → `work`. ✅ Implemented as `lev-ralph`.
- **omx-06** `[OMX]` — Machine API (programmatic mode control) → **MISSING**. Lev uses CLI only.
- **omx-09** `[OMX]` — Auto-nudge / autopilot → partial (`autodev-loop`).
- **omc-01** `[OMC]` — Claude Code team shell → partial. Lev uses Claude Code as adapter, not team surface.
- **omc-03** `[OMC]` — Provider-specific optimizations → partial.
- **ooo-07** `[Ouroboros]` — PAL Router (3-tier cost optimization) → implemented by `lev-ralph` (haiku/sonnet/opus tiering).
- **ooo-08** `[Ouroboros]` — **Ralph persistent loop** across sessions → implemented by `work` (same name even).
- **ooo-09** `[Ouroboros]` — Event sourcing with checkpoint recovery → implemented (LevEvent bus + JSONL).
- **omo-02** `[OMO]` — Planner/executor split → implemented (plugins/core-sdlc/flows/).
- **hm-03** `[Hermes]` — ACP editor integration (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains) → **MISSING**.
**Agent Roles in Deliver (7):** executor, debugger, build-fixer, team-executor, code-simplifier, qa-tester, test-engineer

props.label: LANE · CLOSE (validation)
props.title: Close — Prove It and Ship
props.subtitle: 11 skills · 8 external features · 6 roles · 25 nodes total

props.content:
**Topic**: Proof, review, docs, handoff, closeout.

**Human says**: "Prove it" / "Close this out"

**Runtime surfaces**: review-readiness, validate-emit-learn, handoff

**Lev Skills in Close (11):**

- **`handoff`** — Session close ceremony, writes workstream state. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`lev-align`** — Validation gate checker (cross-appears Plan + Close). **Overlaps:** ooo-10. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`verification-before-completion`** — Evidence before assertions. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`requesting-code-review`** — Pre-merge review. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`receiving-code-review`** — Feedback processing. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`codebase-audit`** — Domain-parameterized audit. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`ubs`** — Static analysis quality gate (Ultimate Bug Scanner). **Origin:** Lev integration.
- **`de-slopify`** — AI writing cleanup. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`finishing-a-development-branch`** — Merge/PR/cleanup decision. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`changelog-md-workmanship`** — Release history. **Origin:** Lev native.
- **`readme-writing`** — README crafting. **Origin:** Lev native.

**External Features Implementing Close (8 — the most framework overlap):**

**The doctor/status/ceremony convergence (5 frameworks agree):**
- **oc-01** `[OpenClaw]` — Doctor command → **MISSING**
- **oc-02** `[OpenClaw]` — Status command → **MISSING**
- **hm-02** `[Hermes]` — Doctor/status health shell → partial (Lev has gates, no consumer shell)
- **omc-02** `[OMC]` — Worker health monitoring and HUD → **MISSING**
- **omx-07** `[OMX]` — HUD status display → **MISSING**

**Other Close features:**
- **ooo-04** `[Ouroboros]` — 3-stage eval gate (Mechanical/Semantic/Multi-Model) → implemented (EvalStrategy has 4 kinds).
- **ooo-06** `[Ouroboros]` — Nine agent personas → ✅ **SUPERSEDED** by Lev's tribunal/cdo/adversarial (explicit rejection).
- **omo-03** `[OMO]` — Hash-anchored edits → **MISSING**.
**Agent Roles in Close (6):** code-reviewer, quality-reviewer, security-reviewer, writer, git-master, performance-reviewer

props.label: INTELLIGENCE · FRAMEWORK OVERLAP
props.title: Where The Frameworks Converge
props.subtitle: Same concept, different names — this is where Lev steals from the best

props.content: **Biggest insight**: The **doctor/status/ceremony** pattern has 5 frameworks converging independently. When 5 external projects all build the same thing, Lev NEEDS it. That's our P0 gap per the adoption matrix. All 5 contribute to the same lane (Close) because they're all about 'prove the system is healthy before you ship'.

props.label: INTELLIGENCE · OUTLIERS
props.title: Where the Graph Disagrees With Itself
props.subtitle: Skills that straddle lanes — candidates for Lane.CROSS

props.content:
**Spike 07 (NetworkX community detection) found 3 outliers** — skills where Louvain, Label Prop, and Greedy Modularity disagree with hand-authored lane assignment:

- **`cdo`** — labeled Shape, but community detection places it in Deliver. It's a deliberation tool used in both alignment AND execution phases.
- **`work`** — labeled Plan, but clusters across Plan + Deliver. It's THE lifecycle orchestrator — spans all phases.
- **`arch`** — labeled Plan, but it's used in Shape (interview) + Plan (ADRs) + Close (review).

**Proposal**: Add `Lane.CROSS` to the enum for multi-lane skills. These three get re-tagged as CROSS. The community detection agreement drops slightly (95.10% → maybe 96%+) and the model becomes more honest about real-world usage.

**Also**: `lev-align` appears in both Plan and Close in the taxonomy (spike 7 found it as the boundary-crossing node). It IS the bridge between crystallization and validation. Keep it dual-tagged or promote to CROSS.

props.label: FRAMEWORKS · SOURCES
props.title: The 7 Frameworks Attributed in This Graph
props.subtitle: Count of features per framework

props.title: OMX (oh-my-codex)
props.content: **9 features.** 4-lane operator, deep-interview, tmux team, RALPLAN, HUD. P0 — most integrated, active worktree at .worktrees/lev-omx.

props.title: Ouroboros
props.content: **12 features.** Socratic interview (0.2 threshold), ontology convergence (0.95), drift score, 3-stage eval, Ralph loop. P0 — most overlap with Lev.

props.title: Lev native
props.content: **46 skills.** Everything with lev- prefix + core skills. The unification layer. 'The brain' per the grab-bag report.

props.title: OMO (oh-my-openagent)
props.content: **3 features.** Category routing, planner/executor split, hash-anchored edits. P2 — smallest overlap.

props.title: OMC (oh-my-claudecode)
props.content: **3 features.** Claude Code team shell, worker HUD, provider-specific. P2.

props.title: Hermes (NousResearch)
props.content: **2 features.** Doctor/status health shell, ACP editor integration. P1 — personal agent runtime.

props.title: OpenClaw
props.content: **2 features.** Doctor command, Status command. Part of the 5-way doctor/status convergence.

props.label: NEXT
props.title: What This Brief Unblocks
props.subtitle: Mixed graph, gen-ui explorer, runtime dispatch

props.content:
**Immediate (Phase 1):**
1. Add NodeKind.FILE, CONTENT, ASSET, BEAD, SPEC, DECISION, HANDOFF to schema
2. Fast-follow ingest walks ~/.agents, docs/, dna/, .lev/, **/*.flow.yaml → ~10k FILE nodes
3. Framework attribution backfill — every skill gets source_project set correctly (currently 0 for skill nodes — only features have it)

**Short term (Phase 2):**
4. Lane.CROSS enum + retag cdo/work/arch
5. Doctor/status skill implementation (P0 — 5 frameworks agree)
6. OMX tmux team lifecycle merge into NTM

**Medium term (Phase 3):**
7. Cytoscape.js explorer with framework-colored nodes (OMX purple, OOO cyan, Lev gold, etc.)
8. Overlap view — highlight features implemented by multiple frameworks
9. Lane drill-down — click lane, see all 25-31 nodes with descriptions

**Published:** https://stellar-sierra-pjgw.here.now/ (prior intelligence dashboard)
**This page:** will publish next.
